mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms synchronized consumer demand expansion, validating corporate earnings trajectories and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 52% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns during expansion regimes, with out-of-sample directional accuracy falling below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer income growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases show no predictive power for 5-day SPX direction, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220 while the federal funds rate remains above 2.0%, persistent inflationary pressures force restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS thresholds are breached.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in industrial production and real GDP confirms a broad-based supply-side economic expansion, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 53% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and impending earnings revisions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns during late-cycle regimes, with out-of-sample directional accuracy remaining at or below 50%.
